description: Bracket-read miss yields not (§59.6 — GAP-1). Reading a map is bracket-native: @m[k] evaluates to ValT | not — a present key yields its value, a key-MISS yields not (§42). @fares is seeded [ "DAL": 4500 ]; @fares["DAL"] hits (4500) and @fares["XXX"] misses. The miss yields not, which renders EMPTY in the DOM (the not-renders-nothing convention). Observed via DOM: #hit shows 'HIT:4500', #miss shows 'MISS:' (the interpolation of the missing key contributes no text).
rationale: §59.6 @m[k] -> ValT | not; a key-miss yields not (composes with given / is some / match). A missing-key bracket-read interpolated into markup renders empty; a present-key read renders its value.
